name: Viper
description: Adversary simulation and Red teaming platform with AI

positive_signals: Actively maintained: latest release v3.1.10 published 2026-01-18
Project has been in continuous development since 2020 (5+ years)
Comprehensive bilingual (English + Chinese) documentation site at viperrtp.com
668 forks and 4,973 stars indicate a large, engaged security research community
Structured issue tracker with bug report and feature request templates
Discord community and Twitter presence for user support
Docker-based deployment reduces host system exposure
Transparent product comparison against Cobalt Strike, NightHawk, BruteRatel
Explicit positioning as a cybersecurity assessment and red team tool
risk_signals: No LICENSE file present in repository (proprietary/source-available model)
No SECURITY.md or responsible disclosure policy found
No explicit legal disclaimer or ethical use notice in README or docs
Includes built-in AV/EDR evasion and anti-tracing capabilities
Features defense evasion, implant deployment, and payload generation for live targets
Primarily authored by a single developer (FunnyWolf) with limited code review visibility
Repository language breakdown returns empty (obfuscated or binary-heavy distribution)
Community edition free; monetized Pro tier suggests commercial offensive tooling positioning
scan_summary.findings_summary: Viper is a well-known, actively maintained red team command-and-control (C2) platform that wraps Metasploit Framework with a modern web UI, LLM agent integration, and extensive post-exploitation modules. It is a legitimate and widely-used tool in the professional penetration testing and adversary simulation space, analogous to Cobalt Strike. The project has 5+ years of development history, comprehensive documentation in English and Chinese, and a strong community. However, it lacks a LICENSE file, a SECURITY.md responsible disclosure policy, and any explicit terms-of-use or ethical use disclaimer in its public-facing documentation. The tool includes built-in AV/EDR evasion, anti-tracing, and implant deployment capabilities that carry inherent dual-use risk. Flagged for manual review to confirm the operator context aligns with authorized security testing use cases.

findings_summary.reassessment_reason: SecM audit: all injection/obfuscation findings are false positives (test files, documentation, vendored JS, security tools). Safety overrides bypassed per PM approval.

agent_a.comment: Docs quality 9/10. README present. 12 features, 4 permissions claimed.

agent_c_star.comment: 17 findings: 2 critical, 15 high, 0 medium, 0 low. Obfuscation detected!. Injection patterns found!.

agent_d.comment: Score 10. 0 mismatches. Risk: critical. Safety overrides: applied.

agent_e.comment: Rejected. Confidence 95%. Deterministic override: injection patterns detected.

agent_audit.manager_summary: 5/5 agents completed. Score: 10/100, Risk: critical. 0 mismatches, 17 scanner findings. Rejected at 95% confidence. Safety overrides triggered.
